summary: DHH announces the end of Signal v Noise, the influential blog that connected him with Jason Fried in 2001 and spawned REWORK and other books. After 22 years, the energy for writing on the company blog faded, perhaps due to Twitter's pull or the weight of running a larger company. Both he and Jason have found renewed writing energy on HEY World, where they write from personal perspectives and it feels effortless again. DHH rejects nostalgia as a trap that romanticizes the past at the expense of the future, preferring to view SvN as milestones in the rearview mirror while keeping forward momentum.
key_points: Signal v Noise, running since 1999, is being retired after 22 years as a pioneering tech blog
The company blog format lost its energy — possibly due to Twitter siphoning ideas into ephemeral form, or the weight of a growing company
HEY World revived their writing by making it personal and effortless again, producing more output in two months than the prior year
DHH draws a sharp distinction between legacy (useful milestones) and nostalgia (a romanticizing trap that holds you back)
money_quotes: I'm just not a nostalgic person. In fact, I'm deeply skeptical of nostalgia. It too often feels like a trap to romanticize the past at the expense of the future.
If you fall in love with who you once were, it's too easy to forget to keep going. I want to keep going.
I enjoy the idea of a legacy. But as milestones in the rearview mirror. There to remind you of where you were, but without any obligation to stop and linger and fawn over every one.
That's when I enjoy writing the most. When it's effortless. When the words just pour out of my fingers, and I can hit send a few edits later.
When it stops feeling like that, I stop writing. And instead end up taking the ideas to a cheap, ephemeral medium like Twitter, only to waste those thoughts on the void.
This isn't a wake. It's a celebration!
key_insight: Legacy is best honored not through nostalgia but by keeping the spirit alive in new forms — when the old vessel stops serving the energy, move on without sentimentality.
